subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear overall structure with title page, table of contents and section headings guiding the reader
Logical sequence of introduction, modelling, analysis and conclusion
Effective division of the bottle into three parts for stepâbyâstep volume calculations
weaknesses: Extensive raw working (e.g. full Gaussianâelimination tables) interrupts narrative flow
Repeated or unnecessary material (e.g. redundant ratio calculations) reduces conciseness
Missing crossâreferences, axis labels and inconsistent table headings hinder readability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the exploration is coherent and wellâorganized (strands at band 3), significant blocks of raw working and some unnecessary repetition limit overall conciseness (strand at band 2). On balance this warrants 2â/â4: the structure is solid, but clarity and relevance issues prevent a higher mark.

strengths: Use of multiple representations (GeoGebra screenshots, tables, algebraic derivations) effectively links abstract maths to real geometry
Correct use of integral and matrix notation in many places
Explanation of row-echelon form and pivot strategy is mathematically accurate
weaknesses: Inconsistent units (cmÂ³ vs cmÂ²) and some corrupted sub/superscripts reduce precision
Variables (e.g. packaging utilization) are not always defined before use
Dense matrix notation and logical leaps (volume ratio application) interrupt clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Notation and terminology are intermittently correct with effective representations (bands 2â3) but lack consistency and clarity in places (band 2 overall). The student shows some good communication, but errors and undefined variables limit the mark to 2â/â4.

strengths: The clay punt model demonstrates clear independent thinking and a hands-on personal approach
The choice of a personally purchased shampoo bottle and environmental motivation shows genuine engagement
Use of home-made scaling and polynomial fitting indicates creativity and initiative
weaknesses: The mathematical techniques (volume of revolution, systems) follow standard textbook methods rather than highly innovative approaches
Opportunities exist to push the inquiry further through novel modelling variants
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands show clear, significant personal engagement (band 2). The investigation is driven by the studentâs own curiosity and hands-on approach, but does not consistently reach the highest level of innovation required for band 3, meriting 2â/â3.

strengths: Clear quantitative comparison between computed capacity and labelled volume
Concrete recommendation (cut 2.09 cm) shows some evaluation of outcome
weaknesses: Reflection is limited to brief comments; modelling assumptions (choice of quintic, data reliability) are not critically examined
No error estimates or discussion of model sensitivity
Surface-level analysis of numerical instability without deeper alternative exploration
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection demonstrates only limited depth and critical analysis (bands 1â2), with minimal examination of assumptions or error. This yields 1â/â3, reflecting basic evaluation but insufficient critical reflection.

strengths: Appropriate and purposeful use of calculus of revolution, Gaussian elimination and unit conversions
Application of quintic polynomial modelling shows advanced mathematics
Generally correct execution with plausible numerical results
weaknesses: Some steps are inefficient and intermediate tables could be condensed
Key logical steps (e.g. deriving radius from perimeter) are not fully explained
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics used is relevant and commensurate with course level, with a good grasp of calculus and linear algebra (bands 4â5). However, minor errors, inefficiencies and insufficient explanation reduce the work to 3â/â6 rather than a top-band score.

strandSelections.reasoning:
Arguments are mostly understandable, but lengthy matrix manipulations interrupt the narrative and some logical leaps (e.g.
directly multiplying by a volume ratio) are not fully explained.  Consequently clarity is uneven, fitting âsome logical structure and clarity.â

Working shows grasp of calculus and matrix methods; numerical answers are plausible and errors are minor (e.g.
ratio inconsistency 13.5/13.7).  Overall understanding is good and largely accurate, meriting 4 marks rather than the flawless higher bands.

The student employs labelled photographs, Geogebra screenshots, tables, algebraic derivations and integrals.  These multiple representations are generally well linked to the text and help the reader visualise the modelling process, so the use is âeffectiveâ (band 3) rather than merely âsomeâ or âhighly effectiveâ.

The investigation follows a clear sequenceâintroduction, three-part volume modelling, punt deduction, utilisation analysis and conclusionâso the reader can follow the mathematical journey without getting lost.  Although algebraic detours are occasionally long-winded, the argument rarely loses its thread and each intermediate result feeds the next step.  This aligns best with âcoherent and logically developedâ rather than the sporadic or exemplary ends of the scale.

Using clay to capture the punt and experimenting with polynomial fittings shows creativity and initiative beyond a routine classroom task, but the modelling strategy itself (volume of revolution) is standard; thus band 2 is appropriate.
The student comments superficially on numerical instability but does not rigorously test alternative models or quantify error, so the analysis is basic rather than thoughtful.

There is a clear comparison between computed capacity and labelled volume, with a quantitative proposal to cut 2.09 cm, showing some evaluation of outcome and implication, but it stops short of comprehensive future recommendations.  Band 2 fits best.

Reflection is limited to a brief summary of findings and a statement that 2.09 cm could be removed.  There is little examination of modelling assumptions (e.g.
why a quintic was chosen) or data reliability.  This matches âlimited reflection present.â

Standard notation (â«, ÏrÂ²h, matrices) is used, but sub- and superscripts are sometimes corrupted and units are mixed (cmÂ³ vs cmÂ²).  Variables are not always defined before use.  This intermittent correctness places the work in âsome appropriate language and notationâ rather than the more consistent higher bands.

The investigation is driven by the studentâs own environmental curiosity and organised around a self-designed three-part model, indicating a clear personal approach, though not an especially innovative one.

While most content is relevant, large blocks of raw Gaussian-elimination working and repeated ratio calculations inflate the length without adding insight.  Some paragraphs restate information already given.  Hence the work is âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elements,â matching band 2.

Calculus of revolution, Gaussian elimination and unit conversions are all relevant to determining bottle capacity and used effectively at multiple stages.  Although some steps are inefficient, the chosen mathematics is appropriate and used with purpose, aligning with the descriptor for 4 marks.

Choosing a personally purchased shampoo bottle, building a clay punt model and devising a home-made scaling method show genuine independent thinking, yet the mathematics itself follows textbook techniques.  This is âsignificant evidenceâ but not outstanding.

A title page, table of contents and section headings give the work a recognisable introductionâbodyâconclusion structure.  Diagrams, data tables and algebra are embedded in the relevant sections.  Crossâreferencing (e.g. âsee figure 8â) is occasionally missing, yet the overall layout is orderly, satisfying the description of "well organised with clear structure".

The mathematics (integration of polynomial functions, 3-D volume scaling) sits squarely at HL level and is generally executed correctly.  It goes beyond SL by incorporating systems of equations and linear algebra, warranting a âfully commensurateâ rating.

annotations.comment: A concise, explicit research question or aim would focus the introduction and clarify the investigationâs objectives.
The table of contents demonstrates a clear overall structure, guiding the reader through each section logically.
Including page numbers next to each entry would improve navigation and strengthen organizational clarity.
The studentâs personal motivation from observing unused volume in a new shampoo purchase is engaging and shows initiative.
Including a brief error estimate for the volume ratio would enhance the depth of analysis.
Using a quintic polynomial to model the upper curve demonstrates advanced application of HL-level mathematics.
The Geogebra curve overlay is effective, but axis labels and scale markers are missing, reducing clarity.
Define the dimensions of the coefficient matrix and vector explicitly (e.g., $$6\times6$$) to improve mathematical communication.
The explanation of row echelon form and pivot strategy is mathematically accurate and clearly communicated.
Extensive raw Gaussian-elimination tables interrupt the narrative; summarizing key steps would improve conciseness.
Several variables (e.g., âpackaging utilizationâ) are introduced without formal definitions; defining terms would improve mathematical communication.
Dividing the shampoo bottle into three parts (upper, middle, lower) provides a clear framework for sequential volume calculations.
When describing the cylinder volume formula, explicitly state $$V=\pi r^2h$$ rather than referring generically to base area Ã height.
The footnote on punt design is informative, but integrating this definition into the main text would improve coherence.
Table headings repeat âPerimeter of middle body partâ twice. Clarifying each columnâs unique purpose would reduce confusion.
Units for each measurement in the table should be labeled consistently (e.g., cm, cmÂ³) to avoid ambiguity.
Explain the step where perimeter divided by Ï yields the radius, to strengthen the logical flow of the calculation.
The clay model of the punt illustrates strong personal engagement and a creative hands-on approach.
Treating the clayâs imperfect base as a perfect circle is a strong simplification; discuss potential error in this assumption.
A brief reflection on the reliability of the clay model measurement would deepen critical analysis.
Rotating and importing the image into GeoGebra provides an effective multiple representation of the curve.
State explicitly that the y-axis serves as the axis of revolution when using the volume integral.
Consider discussing how image processing (Photoshop) might introduce distortions affecting curve fitting.
The combination of Geogebra screenshot and cylinder volume table effectively links abstract mathematics to physical dimensions.
The volumeâratio calculation $$642\div47.0$$ is stated as 13.7; clarify rounding strategy since exact ratio is 13.66.
Consider justifying the choice of quintic rather than a lower-degree polynomial based on goodness of fit metrics.
Translating the point data into six simultaneous equations is correct, but the notation is dense; consider matrix notation to streamline presentation.
Rounding input points to three significant figures may introduce truncation error; note this in critical reflection.
Consider presenting a single condensed matrix progression rather than full intermediate tables to enhance readability.
